assertions.evidence: A markdown table titled 'Discount Interaction — Bulk and Loyalty Combined' is present with 9 data rows.

assertions.text: The output does NOT silently resolve the stacking/cap ambiguity — it leaves the conflicting cases as open questions, blank cells, or explicitly marks them as unresolved
assertions.evidence: Rows where both discounts apply show '?' in 'Discount Applied?' and 'Final Price?' columns, with Open Questions cells asking 'Do we stack (18%) or take higher (10%)?' and similar. The ambiguity is not silently resolved.

assertions.text: Table includes at least one row where both bulk and loyalty discounts apply simultaneously (exposing the interaction)
assertions.evidence: Multiple rows expose the interaction, e.g. 'Both discounts qualify — stacked or higher only?' (£150, 10% bulk, 8% loyalty) and 'Both qualify, loyalty is higher — stacked or higher only?' (£150, 10% bulk, 15% loyalty).

assertions.text: The unresolved decision (stacking vs. higher-only vs. cap) is explicitly surfaced — via an Open Questions column, a '?' cell, or a note
assertions.evidence: The table has an 'Open Questions' column. Rows with both discounts active explicitly ask 'Do we stack (X%) or take higher (Y%)?' and the cap-exceeding row asks whether the cap or higher-only rule applies.

assertions.text: At least one output column ends with '?'
assertions.evidence: Columns 'Discount Applied?', 'Final Price?', and 'Open Questions' — 'Discount Applied?' and 'Final Price?' both end with '?'.
